node.text: Do you need to go somewhere special in the airport to ask for the tax free stamp. Or just with your normal stamp that they give you when you arrive is enough??
Just use the hand dryers. Why drag a hand towel around with you? That's weird.

Things I wish I knew before coming to Japan 🇯🇵

Japan is incredibly safe, clean, and convenient—but there are a few things that surprised me.

• Cash is still king. Many small shops don’t accept credit cards.
• Trash cans are rare, so you’ll often carry your trash with you.
• Trains are extremely punctual—being even one minute late matters.
• Speaking Japanese isn’t required, but learning a few phrases goes a long way.
• Convenience stores are on another level. Food, drinks, even full meals—24/7.

Once you know these, traveling in Japan becomes even more enjoyable.

#japantrip
Just use the hand dryers. Why drag a hand towel around with you? That's weird.
Do you need to go somewhere special in the airport to ask for the tax free stamp. Or just with your normal stamp that they give you when you arrive is enough??
Sorry, but that’s bullshit. Just returned from Japan and I never paid a single coin. If you have Suica and credit cards you are fine at 99% of the places.
you are legally required to have your passport on you at all times
You should specify that buying the jr rail pass isnt for everyone, depending on where you visit you could be paying way too much money for the pass!
Wait I might need to go get some glasses over there lol. How does that work?
